P4-001.title: Perplexity acquires Read.cv, a social media platform for professionals
P4-001.author: TechCrunch staff
P4-001.excerpt: Andy Chung: 'We've long admired Perplexity and believe great things happen when the world's knowledge is made more open and accessible.' Aravind Srinivas: 'The team is incredibly capable in designing and building consumer and social experiences and we look forward to working with them on many new exciting directions!' Wind-down begins Jan 17, 2025; data export deadline May 16, 2025; .cv domains migrating to Hello.cv from Jan 31, 2025. Read.cv founded 2021 by Andy Chung (ex-Facebook, Mozilla, Quip), ~3 employees at shutdown. Perplexity's third acquisition after Carbon and Spellwise.
P4-001.relevance: Primary source for Read.cv shutdown facts and acqui-hire framing
P4-001.credibility: high — TechCrunch primary reporting with direct quotes

P4-002.title: Andy Chung shutdown announcement on posts.cv
P4-002.author: Andy Chung
P4-002.excerpt: Hey all, I wanted to share a very bittersweet update here before we shared it anywhere else. First, the hard part — we've made the difficult decision to wind down Read.cv and Posts starting this month. To be transparent, the reason for this decision is anticlimatic — ultimately the challenges of sustaining a small business were more than we could overcome.
P4-002.relevance: Founder's stated reason for shutdown — frames Read.cv death as small-business-economics failure rather than product failure
P4-002.credibility: high — primary source, founder's own platform

P4-003.title: R.I.P. Read.CV — The Forever LinkedIn Competitor
P4-003.author: Patricia Parnet
P4-003.excerpt: Described as 'a show don't tell platform' celebrating creative work. Featured 'digital gardens, independent film, zines, furniture making, self-published music' alongside professional profiles. Called itself 'a calm place for everyone' without commercial pressure. 'Minimalistic and yet so beautiful' but the author acknowledges it remained 'niche product serving creatives rather than the broader professional market'. 'We don't just want another LinkedIn. We want something better.'
P4-003.relevance: Eulogy article — captures the niche-design-quality positioning of Read.cv and acknowledges the scaling problem
P4-003.credibility: medium — opinion piece but from engaged former user

P4-004.title: Eulogy for a product that really cared: read.cv and posts.cv say goodbye
P4-004.author: Facundo Matiaude
P4-004.excerpt: Genuine interaction happened organically; new users received real responses to first posts. 'Super organized layout, a clean focus on content, no clutter or ads all over the place.' 'Niche, meaningful conversations instead of transactional interactions.' Community embodied 'a culture of trust — where quality mattered more than quantity'. Author theory: 'can't have your cake and eat it too' — beloved small-scale product gets acquired by larger company because growth pressures make human-centered platforms unable to remain independent.
P4-004.relevance: Articulates the quality-vs-growth contradiction explicitly — that the qualities Read.cv users loved were structurally incompatible with the business model needed to survive
P4-004.credibility: medium — opinion but well-articulated user perspective

P4-005.title: Peter Johnston announcement of Polywork shutdown
P4-005.author: Peter Johnston
P4-005.excerpt: Hey folks, I wanted to share some news today that after 3.5 years, I made the call to shut @Polywork down and return the remaining capital to investors. Working on Polywork, especially in the first 18 months was the highlight of my career. The world still needs a new standard in professional identity and discovery.
P4-005.relevance: Founder's primary statement; explicitly returning capital to investors signals the project is being honorably wound down rather than acqui-hired
P4-005.credibility: high — primary source statement from founder

P4-006.title: Peter Johnston sets up new AI start-up as he winds down Polywork
P4-006.author: The Currency
P4-006.excerpt: After 3.5 years, Peter Johnston made the call to shut Polywork down and return the remaining capital to investors. The company was unable to sustain its growth and no longer saw a viable path to Polywork becoming a gigantic business. Polywork raised a total funding of $44.5M over 3 rounds from 41 investors. Johnston is now in the process of building a new start-up and is a co-founder of Profile.com, a San Francisco-based business.
P4-006.relevance: Confirms $44.5M total funding and that the founder is making a SECOND attempt at professional identity (Profile.com) — signal that experienced founders still see opportunity
P4-006.credibility: high — Irish business publication; founder is Irish so likely accurate sourcing

P4-007.title: What Happened To Polywork? An Eye-opening Story Of Fail Growth
P4-007.author: Elena (promptledproduct)
P4-007.excerpt: Five failure factors: 1) Underestimating network effects — struggled to achieve critical mass; 2) Lack of clear differentiation — failed to articulate compelling reasons to choose it over LinkedIn; 3) Pivot confusion — shift to website builder alienated early adopters; 4) Funding pressure — $13M drove unsustainable growth strategies; 5) Market timing — launched during 'tech fatigue' when users were selective about new platforms. User comment Benedikt Kantus: 'They never provided sufficient additional value over LinkedIn. Even if they had become a strong competitor, it would be easy for LinkedIn to copy their few benefits.'
P4-007.relevance: Comprehensive retrospective on Polywork; the Kantus comment crystallizes the 'why not just use LinkedIn' problem
P4-007.credibility: medium — analyst opinion piece, but careful and well-referenced

P4-008.title: Investing in Polywork (a16z)
P4-008.author: Andreessen Horowitz
P4-008.excerpt: a16z investment thesis: framed Polywork as the LinkedIn successor for the era of multi-hyphenate work. The polyworker identity was supposed to replace the corporate-driven 'one job title' identity of LinkedIn. Initial product had activity-feed approach with badges.
P4-008.relevance: Original investor thesis — important context for what Polywork was supposed to be vs what it became
P4-008.credibility: high — primary source from lead investor

P4-009.title: Polywork — Company Profile (Tracxn)
P4-009.author: Tracxn
P4-009.excerpt: Polywork raised a total funding of $44.5M over 3 rounds from 41 investors. Investors include Andreessen Horowitz, Collison brothers (Stripe), Alexis Ohanian, Elad Gil. Shut down January 31, 2025.
P4-009.relevance: Definitive funding totals and shutdown date

P4-010.title: Get paid for freelancing commission-free | Contra
P4-010.author: Contra
P4-010.excerpt: With Contra you can easily accept global payments for your freelance projects commission-free, and users keep 100% of what they earn freelancing on Contra. A $5,000 project on Upwork would result in losing $1,000 to fees; on Contra you keep the full $5,000.
P4-010.relevance: Primary product positioning — commission-free as wedge

P4-011.title: Independent worker network Contra goes commission-free as it secures new NEA-led capital
P4-011.author: TechCrunch
P4-011.excerpt: Contra raised $30M Series B led by NEA in November 2021. Total funding ~$45M. Launched commission-free model alongside the Series B announcement.

P4-012.title: Contra Review: A New Age Third-Party Freelance Platform
P4-012.author: Vettted
P4-012.excerpt: Criticism centers on access to jobs with some users describing a slow, confusing approval process, required portfolio videos, long waits, unclear rejections, and fewer opportunities than expected. The biggest criticism is not enough jobs — on Fiverr and Upwork you pay for access but also for an audience, while on Contra you need to bring your own. Original free-access promise eroded — users joined because it didn't charge to apply for projects, but now access requires being accepted to the 'Invite Only' Contra Job Network.
P4-012.relevance: Central user complaint pattern: 'bring your own client' and access friction — articulates the structural problem with commission-free model
P4-012.credibility: medium — review blog but specific and consistent with other sources

P4-013.title: Contra Reviews on Trustpilot
P4-013.author: Multiple users
P4-013.excerpt: Multiple reports of accounts banned without explanation; portfolio visual chaos characterized as 'Times Square on LSD' by one reviewer; positive feedback: 'polished profiles, proposals, contracts, invoicing, payments, supportive community.'
P4-013.relevance: Aggregated user sentiment — captures both the platform's strengths and the moderation/ban friction
P4-013.credibility: medium — review aggregator, biased sample but large N

P4-014.title: Wellfound (formerly AngelList Talent) Reviews on Trustpilot
P4-014.author: Multiple users
P4-014.excerpt: Wellfound/AngelList used to be a good place to find employment but has started feeling more and more like a graveyard. Companies on the platform are now updating their listings far less frequently. A job board where 80% of applications expire unseen isn't just inefficient — it's an invisible confidence killer. Reports of listings that exist for investor optics, not to hire anyone.
P4-014.relevance: Key 'graveyard' framing of Wellfound — captures the demand-side collapse mechanism
P4-014.credibility: medium-high — large N user reviews, consistent over multiple time points

P4-015.title: Wellfound Review: Is the AngelList Job Board Worth It? (2026)
P4-015.author: Remote Job Assistant
P4-015.excerpt: Wellfound's Android app was removed from the Google Play Store on March 8, 2024, and there is no iOS job-search app. The platform is browser-only in 2026. The salary filter has a critical flaw: activating it hides all listings that don't include a salary range. Since most startups don't post compensation publicly, turning on this filter eliminates the majority of the catalog.
P4-015.relevance: Confirms mobile app death and structural product flaws
P4-015.credibility: medium — niche review site but specific verifiable facts

P4-016.title: The State of UK Tech Hiring — Growth Trends Report Winter 2025
P4-016.author: Cord
P4-016.excerpt: Cord indexes over 5,000 UK tech companies and their active job postings. The platform ranks companies by headcount growth over the past twelve months, with subcategories highlighting industries including FinTech, AI, Data & Analytics, E-commerce & D2C, InsurTech, Security, HealthTech, Sustainability, Blockchain, and Consultancy.
P4-016.relevance: Confirms Cord's scope and positioning — B2B sourcing tool, not consumer network

P4-017.title: Hiring Assistant, LinkedIn's first AI agent for recruiters, now globally available
P4-017.author: LinkedIn News
P4-017.excerpt: Vincent Mercandetti (Siemens): 'Instead of spending an hour sourcing for one project, I can now source candidates for 5 or more projects in 10-15 minutes.' Michael Korn (Expedia Group): 'After I refined my intake notes, I was amazed — three candidates moved to interviews just like that.' Dori Saad (MediaNews Group): 'Hiring Assistant feels like working with a seasoned recruiter, while other AI recruiting tools feel like you're training someone new.' Joe West (Insite): 'They can't match the depth, reliability, or freshness of LinkedIn's information.' Early adopters save 4+ hours per role, review 62% fewer profiles, see 69% higher InMail acceptance.
P4-017.relevance: Primary source for LinkedIn's published Hiring Assistant performance numbers and customer endorsements
P4-017.credibility: high — primary source, but note self-selected customer quotes

P4-018.title: LinkedIn Enters AI Agent Race with LinkedIn Hiring Assistant
P4-018.author: Josh Bersin
P4-018.excerpt: Hiring Assistant 'automates approximately 80% of the pre-offer recruitment workflow'. Two architectural innovations: Experiential Memory (stores recruiter search history, learns individual style) and Project Memory (consolidates info about a single search). 'By embedding an agent directly into LinkedIn's existing workflow, the company leverages its unmatched candidate database and recruiter user base. The platform integration creates switching costs and network effects that pure-play automation vendors cannot match.' Critical: 'As AI helps recruiters source and screen candidates, the candidates are using AI to power-up their resumes. This necessitates better differentiation tools.'
P4-018.relevance: The single best analyst framing of LinkedIn's AI moat AND the 'AI arms race' on both sides of hiring

P4-019.title: LinkedIn Hiring Assistant vs. modern AI sourcing: a recruiter's guide
P4-019.author: Metaview
P4-019.excerpt: LinkedIn Hiring Assistant primarily evaluates structured profile data within the LinkedIn ecosystem; it has limited visibility into broader signals. It does not deeply analyze portfolio work, open-source contributions, nuanced skill adjacencies, or cross-platform activity in a comprehensive way. Total cost per recruiter routinely in five-figure annual territory.
P4-019.relevance: Industry-acknowledged blind spots of LinkedIn Hiring Assistant — portfolio/OSS/cross-platform data is where alternatives can live
P4-019.credibility: medium — competitor source with bias, but specific and corroborated by ERE Media

P4-020.title: 50%+ of LinkedIn Posts were Likely AI in 2025 + Engagement Insights
P4-020.author: Originality.AI
P4-020.excerpt: 53.7% of long LinkedIn posts were classified as Likely AI (confidence ≥ 0.5) in 2025 study. Methodology: 99 influential LinkedIn profiles, Jan-Nov 2025, 3,368 long-form posts (100+ words) via real-time LinkedIn API. Industry breakdowns: Design and architecture = 100% Likely AI; Wellness and personal development = 92% Likely AI. Most industries above 50%.
P4-020.relevance: Quantifies the AI slop crisis on LinkedIn — the central tailwind for an alternative
P4-020.credibility: medium-high — vendor-conducted but methodologically described and widely cited

P4-021.title: LinkedIn has verified 55 million users to combat AI misinformation
P4-021.author: CNBC
P4-021.excerpt: LinkedIn has verified 55 million users as of October 2024. Verified profiles get 60% more profile views, 30% more connection requests, 50% more post engagement.
P4-021.relevance: LinkedIn's identity-verification moat scale — primary number for the verification-as-defense thesis

P4-022.title: Sociable: LinkedIn outlines measures to combat scam job listings
P4-022.author: HR Dive
P4-022.excerpt: LinkedIn's VP of Product Oscar Rodriguez announced new updates including improved scam detection, new verification requirements, AI-powered resume tools and more. LinkedIn has increased its trust signals, including new requirements around workplace verification, which will provide more assurance on job listings.
P4-022.relevance: Confirms LinkedIn is actively expanding the 'Verified' moat into job listings and workplace verification — claiming the trust high ground
P4-022.credibility: high — HR industry publication of record

P4-023.title: LinkedIn launches its first AI agent to take on the role of job recruiters
P4-023.author: TechCrunch
P4-023.excerpt: LinkedIn launched Hiring Assistant as its first AI agent on October 29, 2024, with 500 charter customers. Designed to automate sourcing, candidate evaluation, and outreach for recruiters.

P4-024.title: Read CV Acquired by Perplexity (Hacker News discussion)
P4-024.author: Hacker News community
P4-024.excerpt: Discussion on Read.cv acquisition by Perplexity. Common sentiments: lament for niche/quality product getting swallowed, skepticism that Perplexity will keep the product alive (acqui-hire), mixed feelings on LinkedIn alternatives generally being doomed. [Specific top comments not directly captured due to API rate-limit during research.]
P4-024.credibility: low — referenced but not directly fetched; cited as broader signal not specific quote

P4-025.title: How Handshake reinvented itself for the AI era and built a $100M segment in 8 months
P4-025.author: AI Native GTM (Substack)
P4-025.excerpt: Handshake laid off 15% of its workforce to fund the pivot into AI training data. The company bifurcated operations into two distinct entities: core recruiting business continued as stable SaaS while Handshake AI operated as 'startup within a startup' with CEO personally recruiting technical leadership and enforcing 'founder mode' — high-intensity, speed-and-execution culture. With 500,000 PhDs and 3 million advanced degree holders creating training data, in eight months they built a new business that hit $50 million in revenue in its first four months and is on track to blow past $100M in the first 12 months. Combined annual revenue projected over $300 million by end of 2025.
P4-025.relevance: Most important strategic signal: existing professional networks pivoting to AI-training-data monetization. Validates the 'consumer surface + B-end data flywheel' thesis with a real $300M ARR example.
P4-025.credibility: high — detailed industry analysis, numbers cross-checked with Sacra and Lenny's Newsletter

P4-026.title: Inside the expert network training every frontier AI model | Garrett Lord (Handshake CEO)
P4-026.author: Lenny Rachitsky / Garrett Lord
P4-026.excerpt: Direct interview with Handshake CEO Garrett Lord. Lord describes the pivot as a near-total re-founding — 'a startup within a startup' — and uses 'founder mode' language. PhDs earn $40-$125/hour creating training data. Handshake AI relies on already-verified academic credentials as differentiator vs general-purpose data labeling platforms.
P4-026.relevance: Primary source for Lord's framing of the pivot; confirms the AI-data-monetization play and the credential-verification competitive advantage

P4-027.title: Indeed, Glassdoor to lay off 1,300 staff amid AI push
P4-027.author: TechCrunch
P4-027.excerpt: Recruit Holdings, parent company of Indeed and Glassdoor, announced layoffs Thursday affecting 1,300 employees in its HR technology segment, or about 6% of that segment's total workforce. The job cuts affect functions across R&D, tech, HR, and sustainability divisions, with most affected functions in the U.S.
P4-027.relevance: Confirms structural decline of job-board incumbents — important context for the post-2025 recruiting landscape

P4-028.title: Indeed and Glassdoor are cutting over 1,000 jobs. The CEO overseeing both companies says 'we must adapt' to AI
P4-028.author: Fortune
P4-028.excerpt: Recruit Holdings CEO Hisayuki 'Deko' Idekoba: 'We must adapt' to AI. The company will 'leverage AI to deliver even greater value and innovation to job seekers and employers globally,' focusing on 'simplifying hiring by building a better job seeker and employer experience using AI.' Glassdoor being integrated into Indeed; Glassdoor CEO Christian Sutherland-Wong stepping down October 1, 2025.
P4-028.relevance: CEO direct statement on AI strategy; confirms Glassdoor losing independence — important signal that even content-rich verticals get absorbed

P4-029.title: Glassdoor, Indeed mass layoffs 2025: job sites slash jobs, AI shift
P4-029.author: Fast Company
P4-029.excerpt: CareerBuilder + Monster filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy on June 24, 2025, just one year after the companies merged. Broader recruiting industry undergoing structural disruption.
P4-029.relevance: Monster bankruptcy = the death of one of the original job-board incumbents

P4-030.title: About Doximity
P4-030.author: Doximity
P4-030.excerpt: Doximity has over 3 million registered members, representing more than 85% of approximately 1 million U.S. physicians, plus large portions of nurse practitioners, physician assistants, and medical students.
P4-030.relevance: Doximity scale — successful vertical professional network

P4-031.title: Doximity Announces Fiscal 2026 Second Quarter Financial Results
P4-031.author: Doximity Investor Relations
P4-031.excerpt: FY2025 total revenues of $570.4 million, representing 20% year-over-year growth. FY2026 guidance: $640-646M. Q2 FY2026: 650,000 prescribers used workflow tools (record); revenue $168.5M (+23% YoY). Clinical AI Suite (Ask, Scribe, Dialer) adopted across 140+ US health systems.
P4-031.relevance: Doximity revenue/growth — the rare profitable vertical pro network at scale

P4-032.title: A.Team Comes Out of Stealth With $60M in Funding
P4-032.author: Insight Partners
P4-032.excerpt: A.Team is the first members-only network of the world's top product builders. $55M Series A co-led by Tiger Global, Insight Partners, Spruce Capital + $5M previous seed = $60M total. Invite-only network of 4,000+ top product builders (later grew to 11,000+). 'Builder Economy as successor of the gig economy.'
P4-032.relevance: A.Team is the largest funded 'elite invite-only network' product builder play. Important precedent for gated/curated professional networks.

P4-033.title: Hire Expert AI Talent | A.Team
P4-033.author: A.Team
P4-033.excerpt: A.Team launched Team Formation AI — generative AI platform enabling tech leaders to build curated teams of Silicon Valley tech talent from the A.Team network, allowing source-and-hire 'in minutes' rather than months.
P4-033.relevance: A.Team's 2024-2025 AI strategy — gated network + AI matchmaking. Closest model to what a 'curated AI-augmented professional network' might look like.

P4-034.title: AI Hiring Under Fire: The Eightfold Lawsuit Explained
P4-034.author: Built In
P4-034.excerpt: Proposed class-action lawsuit filed against AI hiring platform Eightfold, alleging the company uses data from LinkedIn, job boards and applicants' internet activity to assess applicants' likelihood for succeeding in a role. Eightfold's 'proprietary global data set is the world's largest, self-refreshing source of talent data,' encompassing 1B+ workers.
P4-034.relevance: Legal frontier: unauthorized aggregation of LinkedIn data for AI hiring is now litigated. Big risk for scrape-based middleware; advantage for consent-based aggregators.

P4-035.title: How SeekOut Compares to Other AI Recruiting Solutions
P4-035.author: SeekOut
P4-035.excerpt: SeekOut aggregates over 600-750 million candidate profiles from LinkedIn, GitHub, research papers, patents, social media. More recent: 1 billion+ profiles across LinkedIn, GitHub, research, patents.
P4-035.relevance: Confirms LinkedIn-spine dependency of AI recruiting middleware
P4-035.credibility: medium-high — company source with self-promotional bias

P4-036.title: HireEz vs. Seekout vs. Findem
P4-036.author: Findem
P4-036.excerpt: Findem's platform continuously ingests and refreshes 1.6 trillion public data points from over 100,000 sources (such as GitHub, ResearchGate, LinkedIn).
P4-036.relevance: Scale of middleware data scraping — paints the existing landscape for enriched candidate data buyers
P4-036.credibility: medium — competitor comparison so biased toward Findem

P4-037.title: Substack User and Revenue Statistics (2026)
P4-037.author: Backlinko
P4-037.excerpt: Substack now has 50 million active subscriptions, with 5 million paid. Over 20 million monthly active subscribers (CEO claim). 5 million paid subscriptions doubled from 2 million in 2024. Gross writer revenue of $450 million in 2025, with nearly 100,000 publications earning money globally as of April 2026 (up from 50,000 in May 2025). Top 10 authors collectively make >$40M/year.
P4-037.relevance: Confirms Substack as proven alternative-identity surface for writers/intellectuals. 5M paying = signal that professional knowledge workers WILL pay for non-LinkedIn personal brand surface.

P4-038.title: How to Create the Perfect GitHub Profile README (Complete Guide for Developers)
P4-038.author: Farhad Rahimi (DEV Community)
P4-038.excerpt: Your GitHub profile is more than just a collection of repositories — it's your developer identity. Your GitHub profile README is your digital business card in the developer world. It's often the first thing recruiters, collaborators, and potential employers see.
P4-038.relevance: Captures developer-community framing of GitHub Profile as identity-of-record
P4-038.credibility: medium — community blog, but consistent with multiple sources

P4-039.title: Best Practices for GitHub Profile README for Engineers
P4-039.author: AI Grants India
P4-039.excerpt: Modern recruitment has shifted from static PDFs to proof of work. Whether you are applying for a Senior SDE role at a Big Tech firm or seeking non-dilutive funding for your AI startup, your GitHub profile serves several functions: Validation of Skills — it proves you can write clean, documented code. Tech-first or open source-friendly companies often take GitHub seriously — sometimes more than a resume. But for more traditional companies, it's just a bonus.
P4-039.relevance: Articulates the 'proof of work > resume' thesis AND its limit (only for tech-friendly orgs). Important asymmetry signal for our research.

P4-040.title: Bento Sunset
P4-040.author: Bento
P4-040.excerpt: Bento.me will shut down on February 13, 2026, more than two years after being acquired by Linktree. After the shutdown, all Bento user and profile data will be permanently deleted, and existing Bento links will redirect to Linktree.
P4-040.relevance: Primary source for Bento shutdown — demonstrates link-in-bio / personal-page consolidation pattern (tool not network)

P4-041.title: Bento to shut down in 2026 as Linktree takes over and offers migration path
P4-041.author: AlternativeTo
P4-041.excerpt: Linktree shutting down Bento mirrors its earlier pattern: in January 2024, Linktree acquired and shut down Koji shortly after the acquisition. Bento links will redirect to Linktree; users get a special Linktree Pro offer.
P4-041.relevance: Confirms the pattern: link-in-bio market consolidates into single dominant player. Anti-pattern for 'personal page as identity' as a startup play.

P4-042.title: How Does Lunchclub Company Work?
P4-042.author: CanvasBusinessModel
P4-042.excerpt: Lunchclub Plus subscription (~$20-$30/month) represented roughly 60% of revenue in 2025. 2025 revenue split: 60% subscriptions, 25% enterprise, 15% boosts. Total ARR estimated in the low-to-mid tens of millions. Pause Mode and gamified Seasonal Challenges contributed to premium churn below 5% during 2025-26. By 2024, approximately 5 million matches confirmed infrastructure capacity. 72% of users chose Lunchclub primarily for time-saving matchmaking (2025 internal survey).
P4-042.relevance: Lunchclub financial reality — niche AI matchmaker can be sustainable but doesn't scale to LinkedIn replacement
P4-042.credibility: medium — third-party business analysis, numbers should be treated as approximate

P4-043.title: RepVue closes $5 million in new funding to transform how sales professionals make career decisions
P4-043.author: PR Newswire
P4-043.excerpt: RepVue closed $5 million in seed financing led by S3 Ventures with significant participation from TDF Ventures and existing investors. Two years after coming out of beta, RepVue users had contributed over 60,000 ratings of 11,000 sales organizations globally.
P4-043.relevance: RepVue scale and funding — illustrates the niche-stays-niche pattern of vertical pro communities

P4-044.title: LinkedIn engagement down? Here's why.
P4-044.author: Mark Schaefer / Schaefer Marketing Solutions
P4-044.excerpt: Average time on LinkedIn dropped from 58 minutes in 2019 to about 30 minutes in 2024-2025 in the US. Active engagement — sharing updates and interacting in comments — has given way to passive browsing. Recent 2025 data: across nearly 400,000 profiles, average visibility dropped about 47%, engagement 39%, follower growth 42%. Median impressions down 65% since 2023.
P4-044.relevance: Quantifies LinkedIn engagement decline — vulnerability window for an alternative

P4-045.title: LinkedIn Is Fighting Back Against AI Slop — and AI Comments
P4-045.author: Entrepreneur
P4-045.excerpt: LinkedIn using 'AI solving AI' systems to detect generic posts, bot comments, and engagement bait. Posts flagged as low-quality not removed but distribution-suppressed. Engagement pods (artificial mutual-liking groups) being dismantled algorithmically.
P4-045.relevance: Documents LinkedIn's algorithmic counter-strategy against AI slop

P4-046.title: Addressing the Threat of Fake Job Candidates
P4-046.author: New York State Bar Association
P4-046.excerpt: Gartner predicts that by 2028, one in four candidate profiles worldwide will be fake. Deceptive candidates exploit AI tools to misrepresent their credentials, skills and experience.
P4-046.relevance: Gartner forecast — the scale of the credibility crisis driving demand for verification
P4-046.credibility: high — bar association publication citing Gartner

P4-047.title: AI's Impact on Hiring in 2025 - Resume Genius Report
P4-047.author: Resume Genius
P4-047.excerpt: 74% of hiring managers have seen AI-generated content in applications. 77% of hiring teams encounter AI-written resumes. 2025 Canva survey found 45% of job seekers used AI for resume writing. iHire 2025: 29.3% of candidates said they used AI to write or customize their resume or cover letter in the past year, up from 17.3% in 2024.
P4-047.relevance: Quantifies AI-resume usage on candidate side — the 'AI vs AI arms race' Bersin describes
P4-047.credibility: medium — vendor report, but corroborated by other studies

P4-048.title: Resume Now Survey: 62% of Employers Reject AI-Generated Resumes Without Personalization
P4-048.author: Resume Now
P4-048.excerpt: 62% of employers reject AI-generated resumes without personalization. 65% of hiring managers believe AI-generated content should always be flagged, but 31% say it depends on the role. Hiring managers are open to candidates using AI — as long as it's clear the applicant made an effort to personalize and refine the content.
P4-048.relevance: Shows hiring-manager backlash against pure AI content — opening for products that signal authentic human work

P4-049.title: Read.cv (Hack Design retrospective)
P4-049.author: Hack Design
P4-049.excerpt: Read.cv reimagined the professional profile for designers and creatives who want something more thoughtful than LinkedIn but less maintenance-heavy than a custom portfolio site. The platform's work history became a curated timeline, projects got beautiful layouts without code, and profiles felt like they were art-directed rather than algorithmically assembled.
P4-049.relevance: Captures Read.cv's design positioning — the 'thoughtful alternative to LinkedIn' niche

P4-050.title: Polywork gets $3.5M to blend professional and social networking
P4-050.author: TechCrunch
P4-050.excerpt: Polywork raised $3.5M led by Andreessen Horowitz to build 'a new modern LinkedIn' designed around multi-hyphenate identity ('engineer + writer + cat dad'). Activity-feed approach with 'badges' (Spoke at conference, Mentored a junior, etc).
P4-050.relevance: Original product positioning at launch — useful contrast with the eventual pivot

P4-051.title: Polywork shutdown discussion (Hacker News)
P4-051.author: Hacker News community
P4-051.excerpt: Discussion thread on Polywork shutdown. Community sentiment: pivot to website builder was always going to fail; LinkedIn challengers are doomed unless they find structural advantage; multi-hyphenate framing was a fad. [Specific top comments not directly captured due to API rate-limit during research.]
P4-051.relevance: Confirms community consensus on Polywork death
P4-051.credibility: low — referenced but not directly fetched

P4-052.title: Handshake revenue, valuation & funding (Sacra)
P4-052.author: Sacra
P4-052.excerpt: Handshake combined ARR projected to exceed $300M end of 2025, with the AI training data arm responsible for the largest growth. Recruiter-side business stable; AI arm growing rapidly with frontier-AI-lab customers.
P4-052.credibility: high — Sacra is a respected private-company research database

P4-053.title: Handshake Acquires Cleanlab in Talent Grab for AI Data Quality
P4-053.author: TechBuzz
P4-053.excerpt: Handshake acquired Cleanlab, a data quality startup, primarily for technical talent in AI data quality engineering. Pattern: incumbent professional networks using acqui-hires to skill-up for the new AI-data monetization model.
P4-053.relevance: Confirms Handshake's strategic posture: building AI-data capability through M&A

P4-054.title: Over ½ of Long Posts on LinkedIn are Likely AI-Generated Since ChatGPT Launched
P4-054.author: Originality.AI
P4-054.excerpt: Longer-form LinkedIn post analysis shows the share of likely-AI content has been rising steadily since ChatGPT's launch (Nov 2022), surpassing 50% mid-2024 and continuing to rise. Counter-trend: authentic, personal long-form posts still outperform on engagement.
P4-054.relevance: Time series confirmation that AI slop is structural, not cyclical

P4-055.title: 11 Best AI Portfolio Generators 2026 (Free + Paid, Tested)
P4-055.author: Taskade
P4-055.excerpt: Taskade Genesis generates a full portfolio from a single prompt, connects to 100+ integrations (GitHub, Dribbble, Figma). Framer AI, Durable, Mixo, Taskade Genesis all support resume-to-portfolio generation. Durable can get a portfolio live in under 60 seconds with zero configuration.
P4-055.relevance: Captures the AI portfolio generator landscape — tools, not networks. Important for what NOT to be.
P4-055.credibility: medium — vendor-promotional but enumerates the landscape

P4-056.title: Peter Johnston sets up new AI start-up Profile.com
P4-056.author: The Currency
P4-056.excerpt: Johnston is now in the process of building a new start-up and is a co-founder of Profile.com, a San Francisco-based business. Signals continued belief in the professional-identity opportunity despite Polywork's shutdown.
P4-056.relevance: Founder making attempt #2 in same space — important signal that opportunity is perceived as real

P4-057.title: Wellfound (AngelList) Review 2026: Bubble or Breakthrough?
P4-057.author: WhatJobs
P4-057.excerpt: A job board where 80% of applications expire unseen isn't just inefficient — it's an invisible confidence killer. There are concerns about fake job postings, with reports of listings that exist for investor optics, not to hire anyone.

P4-058.title: 'It's a miraculous solution for recruiters': LinkedIn Hiring Assistant redefines recruitment in 2026
P4-058.author: HRD Asia
P4-058.excerpt: Hiring Assistant drawing praise from leaders including HR analyst Josh Bersin who stated 'Hiring Assistant brings all this together into an integrated solution... I would imagine recruiters would save 30 to 40 to 50% of their time, the quality of hire will go up, the time to hire will [improve] and the candidates will have a better experience.'
P4-058.relevance: 2026 confirmation that Hiring Assistant continues to be praised by HR analysts

P4-059.title: LinkedIn Recruiter AI Features: What's New in 2026
P4-059.author: Pin
P4-059.excerpt: By end of 2025 / early 2026: 12 confirmed AI features on LinkedIn Recruiter (up from 1 in early 2024). February 2026 quarterly update: AI Applicant Targeting, AI Follow-Ups, MS Teams integration, Verified Applicant Spotlight.
P4-059.relevance: Confirms LinkedIn's AI feature velocity — 12x increase in 2 years signals strategic seriousness

P4-060.title: Read.cv shutdown — community migration attempts
P4-060.author: Varun Raghunathan
P4-060.excerpt: Read.cv is shutting down, so we decided to build an [alternative]. Multiple community-led attempts to clone/preserve Read.cv emerged in the weeks after the shutdown announcement. Sentiment: 'people were writing saddened posts, trying to create groups to replicate posts.cv, publishing memes of digital grief, and trying to choose whether to migrate the community to Bluesky or some other low-key social platform.'
P4-060.relevance: Captures the community grief / migration scramble — illustrates how niche networks die without clear replacement
